steps.title: Identify the decisive requirement
steps.content: The goal is to specify WHO may have first populated the Azores, according to the 2015 study — so the answer must directly name the specific group the study identified.

steps.title: Find the choice that directly names the group
steps.content:
Choice D — According to a 2015 study, the first humans to populate the Azores may have been Vikings from Scandinavia, not mariners from Portugal as previously believed. directly identifies "Vikings from Scandinavia" and attributes this to "a 2015 study," fully answering the WHO question while contrasting with the previous Portuguese theory.

The other choices fail to do this:
  • A emphasizes the old historical belief
  • B only negates Portuguese involvement without naming Vikings
  • C focuses on the genetic mouse evidence rather than directly identifying who populated the islands
That matches Choice D — According to a 2015 study, the first humans to populate the Azores may have been Vikings from Scandinavia, not mariners from Portugal as previously believed..
